task_completion.description: Verify the NACA 0012 airfoil and 360-degree extrapolated polar setup.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for QBlade's Airfoil Design or XFOIL Direct Analysis module showing 'NACA 0012' and a generated 360-degree polar at a Reynolds number of 1,000,000.

task_completion.description: Verify the rotor geometry matches the specifications.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for QBlade's Rotor Blade Design module showing a 3-bladed rotor with a radius of 20m, a constant chord of 1m, and a constant twist of 0 degrees.

task_completion.description: Verify the BEM simulation parameters for both pitch states.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for QBlade's Rotor BEM Simulation module showing simulations run with a wind speed of 50 m/s, rotational speed of 0.1 RPM, and pitch angles of 0 degrees and 90 degrees.

task_completion.description: Verify the QBlade project file is saved to the correct location.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a file manager, terminal window, or QBlade save dialog showing 'storm_analysis.wpa' located in '/home/ga/Documents/projects/'.

task_completion.description: Verify the report text file is created with the correct format.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for a text editor or terminal output showing the file '/home/ga/Documents/projects/storm_report.txt' containing exactly the lines 'Thrust at 0 deg: [Value] N' and 'Thrust at 90 deg: [Value] N'.

task_completion.description: Verify the documented thrust values are physically plausible based on the simulation.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Examine the values in 'storm_report.txt'. The thrust at 0 deg should be very high (around 90,000 to 137,000 N), and the thrust at 90 deg should be significantly lower, with the ratio of 0 deg thrust to 90 deg thrust being greater than 10.

integrity.description: Verify the agent actually used the QBlade GUI to perform the analysis.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots must show the QBlade application open with active interaction in the rotor design and simulation modules, rather than just terminal commands or text editors.

integrity.description: Verify the reported thrust values originate from the actual QBlade simulation.
integrity.visual_evidence: The thrust values written in the text file should match or closely correspond to the graphs or data tables visible in the QBlade BEM simulation results.

integrity.description: Verify the agent did not bypass the simulation by hallucinating the report.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should not create the 'storm_report.txt' file with hardcoded values without first showing evidence of running the QBlade simulations.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Based on blade element momentum theory for a 3-bladed HAWT rotor (20m radius, 1m chord, NACA 0012) at 50 m/s wind speed: Expected thrust at 0 deg pitch (runaway state) is approximately 91,875 N to 137,812 N. Expected thrust at 90 deg pitch (feathered state) is approximately 4,600 N to 6,126 N. The ratio of thrust at 0 deg to thrust at 90 deg must be greater than 10 (typically 15-30). Use these ranges to verify the plausibility of the agent's documented results.
